title: boolean
text: boolean Uniface data type that holds a Boolean value of 0 or 1 . boolean ParameterName : Direction boolean VariableName Description The Boolean data type is interpreted as either TRUE or FALSE . An empty value, and the values 0 , F , f , N , and n are interpreted as FALSE . All other values are interpreted as TRUE . params boolean pCreditApproved : INOUT endparams Copy Related Links Data Types Data types are classifications for data containers, such as fields, variables, parameters, and return values. They define the characteristics of the data and determine how the data is handled during processing and storage. Data Handling in ProcScript The ProcScript language enables you to convert data from one type to another, perform arithmetic operations, extract data values, and perform string substitution. Uniface carries out implicit type conversion whenever necessary, for example, when assignments are made between non-matching data types or when comparing non-matching data types. The ProcScript language also provides a number of functions that allow you to convert from one data type to another. Packing Codes for Boolean Data The default packing code for Boolean data is B, but there are a number of other codes available. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
